metadata.goal: The student can shade one part to represent a unit fraction

steps.dialogue: Look at this rectangle bar. First, divide it into two [vocab]equal parts[/vocab].

step.dialogue: Check your spacing. You need two [vocab]equal parts[/vocab].

events.name: sequential_division_strategy
events.description: Sequential division strategy shown - midpoint of bar pulses to indicate where equal division should occur

step.dialogue: [event:690422d8aed01e36]Let's think about this together. For [vocab]halves[/vocab], [event:690422c37a475212] the bar needs two parts that are exactly the same size.

events.name: demonstrate_equal_division
events.description: Animated demonstration showing cut line appearing at midpoint, then measurement lines confirming both sections are equal width

step.dialogue: [event:690422d8aed01e36]This can be tricky, so let's work through it together. To make [vocab]halves[/vocab], we cut right in the middle [event:6904230f4355ca2e] so both parts are the same size. See how the left part [event:690423257e77408e] and right part are equal? That's how [vocab]halves[/vocab] work - two [vocab]equal parts[/vocab] make a [vocab]whole[/vocab].

on_correct.dialogue: You divided the bar into two [vocab]equal parts[/vocab].

steps.dialogue: Now shade one part to show [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one-half[/fraction].

step.dialogue: Almost. Shade just one part of the bar.

events.name: outline_single_piece
events.description: One piece of the bar is outlined to show which single section should be shaded

step.dialogue: [event:6949a91ff2a27877] You're working on it. Here's what helps - [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one-half[/fraction] means only [event:6903cc472efadabe] one piece gets shaded, not both.

events.name: demonstrate_shading_one_part
events.description: Animated demonstration showing one section being shaded while the other remains unshaded, with annotation showing '1 out of 2'

step.dialogue: [event:6904237c87a9d97b]Let me help you with this one. [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]One-half[/fraction] means we shade one out of the two parts. Watch as I shade just this one piece [event:6903cd833a710194] . See? One piece shaded, one piece blank. That's how [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one-half[/fraction] works - 1 out of 2 equal sections.

on_correct.dialogue: You shaded one part to show [fraction numerator=1 denominator=2]one-half[/fraction].

metadata.goal: The student can shade one part to represent a unit fraction

steps.dialogue: Do you see this rectangle bar? Divide it into 6 [vocab]equal parts[/vocab], then shade one of them.

step.dialogue: Not quite. Make sure you divide into 6 [vocab]equal parts[/vocab].

events.name: sequential_division_strategy
events.description: Sequential division strategy shown - midpoint of bar pulses to indicate where equal division should occur

step.dialogue: [event:690423d0af417bdf]Let's think about this together. You need 6 [event:690423a5b3f6e932] [vocab]equal parts[/vocab] in the bar.

events.name: demonstrate_equal_division
events.description: Animated demonstration showing cut line appearing at midpoint, then measurement lines confirming both sections are equal width

step.dialogue: [event:690423d0af417bdf]This can be tricky, so let's work through it together. We divide the bar into 6 [vocab]equal parts[/vocab] by making 5 cuts. [event:690424733013f873]One in the middle, then two more in each half.

steps.dialogue: Now shade one part to show one-sixth.

prompt.text: Shade one part to show one-sixth.

step.dialogue: Shade just one part of the bar.

events.name: outline_single_piece
events.description: One piece of the bar is outlined to show which single section should be shaded

step.dialogue: [event:6903cfceacc9f803] You're almost there. Here's what helps - [fraction numerator=1 denominator=6]one-sixth[/fraction] means only [event:6903ce877cefff17] one of the six pieces gets shaded.

events.name: demonstrate_shading_one_part
events.description: Animated demonstration showing one section being shaded while the other remains unshaded, with annotation showing '1 out of 2'

step.dialogue: [event:6903cfceacc9f803] Let me help you with this one. [fraction numerator=1 denominator=6]One-sixth[/fraction] means we shade  one out of the six parts. Watch as I shade just this one piece.[event:6903ce877cefff17]  See? That's how [fraction numerator=1 denominator=6]one-sixth[/fraction] works - 1 out of 6 equal parts.

on_correct.dialogue: Good. You shaded one part to show one-sixth.
